methods.description: This method involves developing a unified probability-regression model to predict pollen concentration based on weather conditions. The model uses daily weather variables and pollen concentration data to create a predictive framework for pollen risks across different regions.

steps.description: Collect daily weather variables and daily pollen concentration data of oak in spring and Japanese hop in autumn from 1997 to 2009.
steps.searchable_summary: gather historical weather and pollen data from 1997 to 2009

steps.description: Represent daily pollen concentration on a histogram according to each weather variable.
steps.searchable_summary: create histograms of pollen concentration against weather variables

steps.description: Fit the histogram to the Weibull probability density function (PDF).
Develop regression models using the PDFs of individual weather variables as independent variables and pollen concentration at all observational sites as a dependent variable.
steps.searchable_summary: develop regression models with PDFs as independent variables

steps.description: Simplify regional regression models into a unified PDF-regression model to predict nationwide pollen risks using numerical weather forecasts.
steps.searchable_summary: simplify and unify regional models for nationwide prediction
